game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Convenience Stories** Players manage a convenience store using a grid-based tap-and-place interface to arrange shelves, product displays, and specialty sections like a newsstand or hot food counter. The core loop involves stocking a diverse inventory of over 150 unique products, from candy to hot meals, to satisfy varying customer demands and generate revenue. Players recruit and assign up to 10 employees, each with different stats, to tasks like stocking shelves or operating the register, ensuring 24-hour service. Progression is driven by accumulating profits to unlock new product types via supplier negotiations, expand the store's physical footprint by purchasing adjacent grid tiles, and upgrade facilities to increase customer satisfaction and store appeal. The game features a competitive element where players must outperform rival stores in sales and customer ratings to "crush" them and expand their franchise territory, with the ultimate goal being to achieve the highest market share and a 5-star store rating.
game_info.description: Welcome to Convenience Stories! Build and manage your own 24-hour convenience store empire. Start with a small shop and expand by stocking products, hiring employees, and satisfying customers. Earn profits to unlock new products, expand your store grid, and upgrade facilities. Compete against rival stores to achieve the highest market share and reach a coveted 5-star rating. Balance inventory management, employee assignments, and customer satisfaction to become the ultimate convenience store tycoon!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate/Select menu options and move cursor on grid
Space: Confirm selection, place items, or interact with highlighted options
Z: Cancel action or go back to previous menu
Shift: Toggle between different view modes (Store/Inventory/Employees)
Enter: Start game from title screen
Escape: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (return to title screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic interaction testing. Tests fundamental game mechanics including menu navigation, product stocking, and basic employee management. Validates that the player can navigate menus, stock shelves, and interact with game systems without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through different menus using arrow keys and space. Stock a few products on shelves. Hire one employee and assign them to a task. Switch between view modes to ensure all interfaces work correctly. Make basic purchases to test transaction flow.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully navigate all menus without errors. Products appear on shelves after stocking. Employee is hired and assigned. View modes switch correctly. Game state updates properly after each action. No crashes or freezes occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition testing. Tests the optimal strategy to reach win conditions by maximizing profits, achieving high customer satisfaction, and reaching 5-star rating. Validates that the game can be won through strategic play.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Rapidly stock high-profit products across all available shelf space. Hire multiple employees and assign them optimally to registers and stocking. Expand store grid as soon as affordable. Prioritize products with high demand. Unlock premium product categories early. Maintain balanced inventory to maximize sales.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Achieve 5-star rating through consistent high performance. Reach target profit threshold. Outperform rival stores in market share. Game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Win screen displays with final statistics showing successful store management.
